_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-9 Airway Management (Revised 12/17/2024, MDHHS Reviewed 2024). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
level_display: Procedure (All EMS Levels — scope varies by intervention)
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-9: Airway Management — MDHHS Approved 12/17/2024
purpose: Govern prehospital airway management across all EMS provider levels. BVM ventilation is the primary standard. Advanced airways are used only when basic airways fail. Sedation to facilitate advanced airway placement is PROHIBITED. MCAs are responsible for training documentation and 100% review of pediatric advanced airways and all cricothyroidotomy procedures.

sections.title: Airway Management Scope by Provider Level
points: MFR: oropharyngeal airway (OPA), nasopharyngeal airway (NPA), bag-valve mask (BVM), suction, colorimetric capnometry

EMT: all MFR skills PLUS CPAP (per 7-5), supraglottic airway (SGA) devices (except i-Gel pediatric), Air-Qsp3/AirQsp3G (> 35 kg), LMA Supreme (adult), King airway (adult), numeric capnometry; waveform capnography per MCA selection
SPECIALIST: all EMT skills PLUS waveform capnography (authorized)

PARAMEDIC: all Specialist skills PLUS i-Gel pediatric, oral endotracheal intubation (ETI), tracheal suctioning, needle cricothyroidotomy, surgical cricothyroidotomy (MCA selection for surgical and commercial percutaneous options)

sections.title: BVM Ventilation Rates
points: Adults > 8 years: 10 breaths per minute (1 breath every 6 seconds)
Children 1–8 years: 20 breaths per minute (1 breath every 3 seconds)
Infants < 1 year: 25 breaths per minute (1 breath every 2.4 seconds)
Two-rescuer BVM is always preferred — one provider maintains mask seal, one delivers breaths
Two-rescuer BVM with supplemental oxygen at 15 LPM
After advanced airway (ETT or SGA with confirmed placement): asynchronous ventilations — adults 10/min; adjust per specific protocol for cardiac arrest vs. other conditions

sections.title: Pediatric Advanced Airway — Restrictive Criteria
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-9: Airway — Pediatric Advanced Airway
points: Pediatric patients should NOT receive an advanced airway (intubation) unless ALL THREE criteria are met simultaneously:
1. Patient has no gag reflex
2. Patient requires sustained positive pressure ventilation (PPV)
3. ALL basic airway interventions AND ALL available supraglottic airway devices have failed
Sedation to facilitate advanced airway placement is PROHIBITED in prehospital EMS in Michigan
Supraglottic airway is the FIRST CHOICE advanced airway when appropriate size is available
ALL advanced airways (ETT and SGA) require ETCO2 confirmation of placement
100% QA/QI review required for all pediatric advanced airways

sections.title: Advanced Airway Documentation Requirements
points: All advanced airway placements must document:
— Device type and size
— Direct visualization of passage through cords (ETT) or placement confirmation method
— Number of attempts
— Tube position in cm at teeth (ETT)
— ETCO2 confirmation type (waveform capnography, numeric capnometry, or colorimetric)
— Serial ETCO2 readings
— Chest rise and fall (bilateral)
— Bilateral lung sounds (confirmed by auscultation)
— Absence of epigastric sounds
— Securing method
— Any complications
— Gastric decompression performed (if applicable)
— Tracheal suctioning performed (if applicable)

sections.title: Supraglottic Airway (SGA) — MCA Selections Required
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-9: Airway — SGA MCA Selections
points: MCA must select ONE primary SGA device
MCA must select AT LEAST ONE additional allowable SGA device
Available SGA options for MCA selection: i-Gel (adult/pediatric), MFR use of i-Gel, Air-Qsp3/AirQsp3G, King airway, Combitube, LMA Supreme
i-Gel pediatric: Paramedic only
All SGA devices require ETCO2 confirmation of placement

sections.title: Cricothyroidotomy — MCA Selections Required
points: Paramedic only
MCA must select one of the following authorized methods:
— None (cricothyroidotomy not authorized)
— Surgical cricothyroidotomy
— Needle cricothyroidotomy
— MCA-approved commercial percutaneous cricothyroidotomy kit
100% QA/QI review required for all cricothyroidotomy procedures

sections.title: Tracheal Suctioning Time Limits
points: Adults > 14 years: maximum 10 seconds per suction attempt
Pediatric patients ≥ 1 year: maximum 10 seconds per suction attempt
Pediatric patients < 1 year (infants): maximum 5 seconds per suction attempt
Hyperoxygenate before and after tracheal suctioning if possible

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select whether EMT-level providers are authorized to use waveform capnography (numeric capnometry is authorized by default for EMTs without this selection)
options: Not authorized at EMT level — Specialist and above only

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select ONE primary SGA device for prehospital use
MCA must select at least one additional SGA device authorized for use (in addition to the primary selection)
MCA must select which cricothyroidotomy method(s) are authorized for Paramedic use

deterioration_flags: ETCO2 = 0 or no waveform with advanced airway in place — ensure adequate CPR/ventilation/circulation; verify tubing connections; if still zero with advanced airway → REMOVE immediately and assist ventilation manually
Failed intubation — attempt SGA; if SGA fails, optimize BVM; consider surgical airway per MCA selection
Pediatric advanced airway — ensure ALL THREE criteria met; 100% QA/QI review required
Cricothyroidotomy performed — 100% QA/QI review required; notify receiving hospital
scope_notes: Sedation to facilitate advanced airway is PROHIBITED in Michigan prehospital EMS
BVM two-rescuer preferred at all levels; BVM rates: adults > 8yr = 10/min; children 1–8yr = 20/min; infants < 1yr = 25/min
Pediatric advanced airway: only when no gag + needs sustained PPV + all basic AND SGA failed
100% QA/QI review: all pediatric advanced airways and all cricothyroidotomy procedures
ETT/SGA placement confirmation: ETCO2 is mandatory
MCA selects primary SGA, additional allowable SGAs, and cricothyroidotomy method
I-Gel (adult): EMT scope at MCAs with i_gel_bls expansion — not in base Michigan EMT scope
